Claude responded: Lots of useful signal.

Lots of useful signal. Three concrete bugs surfaced:

1. Hardcoded "Ms. Jarvis" identity in the system prompt (line 1410):

"I am Ms. Jarvis, your Appalachian AI assistant. "  # identity lock prefix

That's why the persona stays "Jarvis" no matter how often you rename to "Allis" elsewhere.

2. Hardcoded fallback user_id cakidd (lines 246, 270, 286):
python

elif user_id == "cakidd" and any(word in msg for word in ["janney", "heritage", "family", "trail"]):
user_id: str = "cakidd"

Your actual user_id from the session is carrie_kidd. The Janney/heritage context only triggers if user_id is exactly "cakidd", which it isn't anymore. So the AaaCPE special-case context for you never fires.

3. RAG server has a Pydantic field-name bug (line 156):
python

embedding = await embed_query(req.message)
AttributeError: 'SearchRequest' object has no attribute 'message'

The endpoint is being called with a JSON body that doesn't match the SearchRequest schema. Caller is sending one field name, the schema expects another. That's why every /search call returns 502 — RAG never returns context, so the LLM has nothing to ground on, and hallucinates names like "Mama Jimena."

Three real, fixable bugs. Let me look at the exact code for each before patching:
bash

# 1. The hardcoded persona identity prefix
sed -n '1400,1430p' ~/msjarvis-rebuild-working/msjarvis-rebuild/services/main_brain.py

# 2. The cakidd hardcoded user_id and AaaCPE function
sed -n '240,295p' ~/msjarvis-rebuild-working/msjarvis-rebuild/services/main_brain.py

# 3. The RAG server SearchRequest schema vs the actual call
docker exec jarvis-rag-server head -180 /app/services/ms_jarvis_rag_server.py 2>&1 | tail -100

Send all three. Then I issue targeted patches:

    swap "Jarvis" → "Allis" in the identity prefix
    broaden the cakidd check to cakidd OR carrie_kidd
    fix the RAG SearchRequest schema mismatch

These three fixes together should:

    Stop persona drift ("Mama Jimena" → recognizes you)
    Restore RAG context (real grounding instead of hallucinations)
    Trigger your Janney/heritage AaaCPE rules

Send the output.

Claude responded: Fix A landed — line 1410 now says "Ms.

Fix A landed — line 1410 now says "Ms. Allis."

I also see something concerning on that same line. Look closely:

"I am Ms. Allis, your Appalachian AI assistant. "  # identity lock prefix\n                "I will now answer based on the conversation history below.\n\n"

The \n                is literal text in the source file — not a real newline. That whole second string after the comment is being interpreted as part of the comment, not as a Python string. Which means the system prompt is JUST "I am Ms. Allis, your Appalachian AI assistant. " and nothing else from that block. The "I will now answer based on the conversation history below" line is dead — Python ignored it because it was after #.

That's a separate bug worth flagging but not blocking us now. Move on to Fix B:

@[app.post](http://app.post)("/search", response_model=RAGResponse)
async def search_rag(req: SearchRequest):
    try:
        embedding = await embed_query(req.query)
    except Exception as e:
        logger.exception("Embedding error in /search")
        raise HTTPException(status_code=502, detail=f"Embedding error: {e}")
    try:
        chroma_raw = await chroma_query(req.collection, embedding, [req.top](http://req.top)_k)
    except Exception as e:
        logger.exception("Chroma query error in /search")
        raise HTTPException(status_code=502, detail=f"Chroma query error: {e}")
    documents, metadatas = _sanitize_chroma_results(chroma_raw, [req.top](http://req.top)_k)
    return RAGResponse(
        status="ok",
        collection=req.collection,
        user_id="local",
        query=req.query,
        n_results=len(documents),
        documents=[documents],
            if m is None:
                m = {}
            documents.append(str(d))
            metadatas.append(dict(m))
            if len(documents) >= top_k:
                return documents, metadatas
    if len(documents) < top_k:
        flat_docs: list[str] = []
        flat_metas: list[dict] = []
        for docs_row in docs_nested:
            for d in (docs_row or []):
                if d is None:
                    continue
                flat_docs.append(str(d))
        for metas_row in metas_nested:
            for m in (metas_row or []):
                if m is None:
                    m = {}
                flat_metas.append(dict(m))
        while len(flat_metas) < len(flat_docs):
            flat_metas.append({})
        documents = flat_docs[:top_k]
        metadatas = flat_metas[:top_k]
    return documents, metadatas

Claude responded: Useful picture now:

Useful picture now:

8092 (jarvis-auth-api): {"status":"ok"}. Healthy. Independent service. Doesn't talk to the chat pipeline at all — no auth references anywhere in the brain logs. That's correct architecture: auth is its own concern, totally separate from the response pipeline.

8018 (jarvis-neurobiological-master): Healthy. Acts as a router — it knows about blood_brain_barrier, i_containers, qualia_engine, consciousness_bridge. Main-brain calls 8018 via NEURO_MASTER_URL=http://jarvis-neurobiological-master:8018.

jarvis-unified-gateway: Listens on host port 18018 → container port 8001. Different from 8018. And it's getting 401 Unauthorized on POST /chat — INFO: 172.18.0.1:50006 - "POST /chat HTTP/1.1" 401 Unauthorized. Something is hitting unified-gateway directly without auth and getting bounced.

The 401 on unified-gateway is suspicious — that's not your portal (your portal goes through main-brain on 8050). Something else is hitting :18018/chat and being rejected. Could be:

    A health check or polling daemon
    An old polling client we didn't disable
